original_abstract: Transition metal cluster complexes serve as invaluable model systems for elucidating fundamental mechanisms in surface chemistry and catalysis. To characterize their intricate geometries, researchers frequently employ gas-phase infrared action spectroscopy, a powerful technique that maps vibrational excitations through high-sensitivity mass spectrometry.
humanized_abstract: Transition metal cluster complexes are important model compounds to elucidate surface chemical processes and catalytic reactions. We determine their complex structures by gas-phase infrared action spectroscopy and study them by highly sensitive mass-spectroscopic detection of their vibrational excitations.

document.output: Transition metal cluster complexes are important model compounds to elucidate surface chemical processes and catalytic reactions. We determine their complex structures by gas-phase infrared action spectroscopy and study them by highly sensitive mass-spectroscopic detection of their vibrational excitations.
